data.title: Amplitude outlines 15% 2026 revenue growth target as AI agents drive 25% of platform queries
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Amplitude (AMPL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Spenser Skates described Q4 as "one of the strongest quarters in Amplitude history," with revenue reaching $91.4 million, up 17% year-over-year, and annual recurring revenue (ARR) at $366 million. The CEO highlighted, "Our go-to-market motion has matured. There is a tighter focus on value-based cases in the enterprise and on expanding with multiproduct deployments."
  • Skates emphasized AI as a structural tailwind, stating, "Over the past 6 months, our Agentic analytics platform has reached a 76% success rate on complex production-grade queries, that is 7x better than a straight text-to-SQL approach."
  • The CEO introduced the launch of global AI agents and the MCP integration, noting, "Teams can now move from insight to action in minutes, not weeks using analytics, cohorts, experiments and messaging in one continuous agenetic workflow."
  • Skates also announced the acquisition of InfiniGrow, an AI-native marketing analytics startup, to strengthen the platform's capabilities.
  • CFO Andrew Casey stated, "The enterprise is now our core growth engine. ARR from our enterprise customer cohort is up 20% year-over-year, with higher retention and expansion rates than the rest of our business."

Outlook

  • Amplitude expects Q1 2026 revenue between $91.7 million and $93.7 million, representing an annual growth rate of 16% at the midpoint. For the full year 2026, revenue guidance is set between $390 million and $398 million—an annual growth rate of 15% at the midpoint.
  • Non-GAAP operating income for Q1 2026 is projected between (-$4.5 million) and (-$2.5 million). Full-year 2026 non-GAAP operating income is expected between $7 million and $13 million.
  • Non-GAAP net income per share guidance for Q1 2026 is between (-$0.02) and (-$0.01), and for the full year, between $0.08 and $0.13.
  • CFO Casey explained, "We are confident we have the right strategy and the right platform to continue to consolidate the fragmented market."

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $91.4 million, up 17% year-over-year. Fiscal year 2025 revenue reached $343.2 million, a 15% year-over-year increase.
  • Total ARR was $366 million at quarter end, up $18 million sequentially.
  • Non-GAAP operating income for Q4 was $4.2 million, or 4.6% of revenue. Net income per share was $0.04 based on 141.5 million diluted shares.
  • Free cash flow in Q4 was $11.2 million or 12% of revenue, while full-year free cash flow set a record at nearly $24 million.
  • The number of customers with over $100,000 in ARR grew to 698, up 18% year-over-year; customers with over $1 million in ARR rose to 56, up 33% year-over-year.
  • Gross margin for Q4 was 77%, flat year-over-year and up 1 point sequentially.

Q&A

  • Taylor McGinnis, UBS: Asked about the evolution and differentiation between Amplitude's agents and third-party agents. CEO Skates responded, "They both use the same underlying infrastructure...the nature and type of questions are the same whether you're asking it from Claude or Slack or whether you're in Amplitudes UI."
  • McGinnis, UBS: Inquired about the lighter upside in the quarter. CFO Casey explained, "Q4 was a great quarter for new logo ARR...there was a large proportion of ARR that was booked later than we've seen in prior quarters."
  • William Fitzsimmons, Piper Sandler: Questioned NRR improvement drivers. CFO Casey noted, "The improvement in net dollar retention was related to our cross-sell capabilities."
  • Robert Oliver, RW Baird: Asked about the new pricing model's predictability. CFO Casey detailed, "Our new pricing and packaging structure rewards our customers now for adding more and more data into the platform so that they're paying marginally incrementally less."
  • Lucas Cerisola, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about new demand from smaller customers and enterprise strategy. CEO Skates stated, "We're doing both. The core thing you want...is the same, whether you're the newest start-up or your 100 year-old long-lasting business."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a constructive but probing tone, frequently seeking clarification on the sustainability of growth, pricing predictability, and the impact of AI-driven adoption. Questions often focused on expansion potential and competitive differentiation.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone, using phrases like "we are confident" and "we have a massive opportunity" both in prepared remarks and responses. The tone was more assertive regarding platform differentiation than previous quarters.
  • Compared to Q3, both management and analysts appeared slightly more bullish, with management increasingly emphasizing structural tailwinds from AI and analysts probing execution details and monetization strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated from Q3 to Q4, with sequential ARR increases rising from $12 million to $18 million.
  • The number of customers with over $100,000 in ARR increased more sharply quarter-over-quarter (up by 45 in Q4 vs. 19 in Q3).
  • Gross margin improved by 1 point sequentially, and free cash flow margin expanded significantly.
  • Management's tone shifted to highlight the success of multiproduct adoption and the launch of AI agents as a driver of future growth, while reinforcing structural improvements in pricing and enterprise focus.
  • Analysts' questions moved from early-stage AI adoption and product innovation in Q3 to scaling, pricing, and long-term platform strategy in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged continued competition from data platform players and the challenge of maintaining performance differentiation, with CEO Skates stating, "Customers always want the most advanced and bleeding edge capabilities."
  • Churn and contract rightsizing were identified as areas of focus, with improved NRR attributed to cross-sell and upsell motion.
  • CFO Casey mentioned, "We will have higher sales and marketing expenses as a percentage of revenue" in Q1 FY '26, reflecting timing of events.

Final Takeaway

Management highlighted one of the strongest quarters in company history, driven by a surge in enterprise adoption, rapid growth in AI agent-driven queries, and the successful launch of a simplified pricing model. The company expects structural tailwinds from AI to accelerate innovation, deepen customer engagement, and drive profitable growth in 2026, with guidance reflecting continued confidence in Amplitude's expanding platform opportunity.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Valneva sees lower 2026 sales as third-party revenue winds down
data.description:
Valneva (VALN) expects total revenue of €155M–€170M in 2026, including product sales of €145M–€160M.

The lower product sales outlook versus 2025 reflects continued growth in core commercial brands, offset by the planned wind-down of third-party sales.

The vaccine maker said it will maintain disciplined cash management, with operating cash burn expected to decline further in 2026 while continuing to fund strategic R&D.

For 2025, Valneva reported preliminary unaudited revenue of €174.7M, up from €169.6M in 2024. Product sales totaled €157.9M, down from €163.3M a year earlier, mainly due to a 42.3% drop in third-party sales to €19.2M.

Cash and cash equivalents were €109.7M as of Dec. 31, 2025, compared with €168.3M at the end of 2024.

CFO Peter Bühler, commented, “2025 challenged our resilience while reaffirming the strength of our team’s disciplined execution. We remained committed to growing our commercial brands and made strong progress across our key R&D programs, despite headwinds around IXCHIQ and the broader geopolitical pressures. As we enter 2026, we look forward to the potentially transformative Phase 3 data readout for our Lyme disease vaccine candidate, while remaining focused on prudent capital allocation, to support shaping and growing our business in line with our strategic vision.”

Separately, Valneva (VALN) renewed Thomas Lingelbach’s mandate as CEO for a further three-year term and confirmed his continued role as a board member.

data.title: Stock index futures fall ahead of a slew of economic reports, Fedspeak
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Thursday as traders faced a busy day with a slew of economic data and Fedspeak.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) fell 0.15%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) slipped 0.24%, and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures (INDU) edged down 0.14%.

The economic calendar includes initial jobless claims, the February Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index, the pending home sales index, and leading indicators, among others.

Federal Reserve speakers, including Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman, Minneapolis Fed Bank President Neel Kashkari, and Chicago Fed Bank President Austan Goolsbee, will speak at different events.

The Fed balance sheet will be released later in the day.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 2.3 basis points to 3.49%, the benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 1.8 basis points to 4.10%, and the longer-end U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) added 1.5 basis points to 4.72%.

Top gainers premarket included DoorDash (DASH) +13.48%, eBay (EBAY) +6.58%, and Occidental Petroleum (OXY) +3.54%. Decliners included Carvana (CVNA) -16.33%, Molson Coors Beverage (TAP) -6.26%, and Charles River Laboratories (CRL) -5.06%.

data.title: AEGON proposes final dividend for 2025 of €0.21 per common share
data.description:
AEGON (NYSE:AEG) proposes final dividend of €0.21/share, bringing total FY25 annual dividend to €0.40/share, up 14% compared with the total dividend per common share over 2024.

If the proposed dividend is approved by the shareholders, Aegon’s shares will be quoted ex-dividend on June 12, 2026. The record date for the dividend will be June 15, 2026, and the dividend will be payable as of July 6, 2026.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: OXY, DASH, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower in the premarket hours of Thursday, parsing hawkish Fed Jan minutes amid Walmart ((WMT)) earnings anticipation.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Figma (FIG) +15% - Shares jumped after strong Q4 results and upbeat guidance. Revenue rose 40% Y/Y to $303.8M, topping estimates, while net dollar retention hit 136%, weekly active users surged 70% Q/Q, and customers with over $1M in ARR climbed to 67. The company guided Q1 revenue to $315M–$317M, above the consensus of $292.5M, and FY2026 revenue to $1.36B–$1.37B, above the consensus of $1.29B, implying roughly 38% annual growth, alongside expected non-GAAP operating income of $100M–$110M.
  • DoorDash (DASH) +13% - Shares surged as strong order growth and upbeat guidance outweighed a Q4 top- and bottom-line miss. Orders climbed 32% to 903M, and GOV jumped 39% to $29.7B, both above expectations, while the Q1 outlook for GOV ($31B–$31.8B) and EBITDA ($675M–$775M) came in strong. Investors looked past softer margins and heavy investment spending, focusing on demand momentum and growth visibility.
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY) +4% - Shares rose after beating Q4 profit estimates, boosting investor confidence with stronger finances and shareholder returns. The company raised its quarterly dividend by over 8% to $0.26 per share and cut debt by $5.8B following the January 2 completion of its OxyChem sale, bringing total principal debt down to about $15.0B and reinforcing its balance-sheet improvement story.

Biggest stock losers

  • Carvana (CVNA) -16% - Shares plunged as investors focused on profitability concerns despite strong Q4 volume and revenue growth. Sales jumped 58% Y/Y to a record $5.6B, and retail units topped expectations, but gross profit per vehicle declined, and wholesale profitability weakened. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $511M but missed estimates, margins narrowed to 9.1%, and the absence of near-term sales guidance added to caution, overshadowing the company’s long-term targets.
  • Avis Budget Group (CAR) -12% - Shares dipped after Q4 results missed expectations, as ongoing repositioning efforts weighed on performance, including another charge tied to reducing the carrying value of its U.S. EV fleet. Management said it is tightening fleet discipline, strengthening the balance sheet, and improving customer experience as it heads into 2026 with a focus on more sustainable earnings growth.

data.title: Microsoft says it doesn’t think ICE is using its tech for mass surveillance
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) said Wednesday it does not believe U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement ((ICE)) is using its technology for mass surveillance of civilians, though it confirmed providing cloud-based productivity and collaboration tools to the agency.

The response followed a Guardian report claiming ICE increased its reliance on Microsoft’s cloud platform as arrest and deportation operations expanded. Citing leaked documents, the report said ICE significantly boosted data storage in Azure and appeared to be using a range of Microsoft's productivity tools, including AI products, to search and analyze that data.

“As we've previously said, Microsoft provides cloud-based productivity and collaboration tools to DHS (Department of Homeland Security, of which ICE is a part) and ICE, delivered through our key partners,” a Microsoft spokesperson said in a statement. “Microsoft policies and terms of service do not allow our technology to be used for the mass surveillance of civilians, and we do not believe ICE is engaged in such activity.”

The company added that Congress, the executive branch and the courts should draw “clear legal lines” on how law enforcement can use emerging technologies.

ICE declined to comment on specific tools used in investigations but said it relies on various technologies to support criminal enforcement. The issue comes amid criticism of President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown, which his administration says is aimed at strengthening domestic security and curbing illegal immigration.

Microsoft (MSFT) has previously faced scrutiny over government use of its technology, including last year when it disabled certain services used by an Israeli military unit following reports of mass surveillance of Palestinian phone calls.

data.title: BE Semiconductor proposes €1.58 dividend for FY 2025
data.description:
• BE Semiconductor (BESIY) proposes €1.58/share dividend for Fiscal Year 2025.

data.title: Remitly outlines 2026 revenue guidance of up to $1.96B as new products and AI boost expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Remitly Global, Inc. (RELY) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Matthew Oppenheimer, CEO, opened the call by announcing the appointment of Sebastian Gunningham as Remitly's new CEO, emphasizing that "We ended this strategically important year with incredible results, growing revenue by 29% and reaching adjusted EBITDA of $272 million in 2025, exceeding our guidance for both." Oppenheimer highlighted the company's transformation over five years, noting "We are now over $1.6 billion in revenue, serving more than 9 million customers." He stated that the company's core money movement business continues to show strong momentum, with less than 4% market share in the consumer total addressable market, providing significant headroom for expansion. Oppenheimer cited the growth of new products, such as Flex (Send Now, Pay Later), which reached around 120,000 users, and Remitly Business, which ended the year with over 15,000 business customers. He also outlined strategic priorities for 2026, including further expansion in high-amount senders and geographical growth in regions like the UAE, Japan, Saudi Arabia, and potentially Brazil.

  • Sebastian Gunningham, the incoming CEO, introduced himself and described his background in product, engineering, and financial services, noting, “Remitly serves a global community that has been historically underserved and overcharged.” Gunningham stressed his focus on building the best product, leveraging AI, and driving growth: “In digital financial services, the product is the business. That will be my focus and the way we keep growing our customer base, delivering a great service and driving our revenue growth.”

  • Vikas Mehta, CFO, stated, “Fourth quarter was $442 million revenue, up 26% year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA was $89 million, resulting in an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20%, our highest quarterly adjusted EBITDA margin ever.” Mehta emphasized efficiency gains, record low transaction losses, and disciplined expense management as key drivers for profitability.

Outlook

  • The company expects Q1 2026 revenue of $436 million to $438 million, reflecting 21% growth. For the full year 2026, revenue guidance is between $1.94 billion and $1.96 billion, or 19% to 20% growth. Mehta said, “We expect revenue from new products to more than double in 2026.” Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2026 is projected between $82 million and $84 million, with a margin of around 19%. For the full year, adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $340 million and $360 million, with a margin of around 18%. The company expects to generate positive GAAP net income each quarter and strong year-over-year growth in GAAP net income and free cash flow.

Financial Results

  • Remitly reported Q4 revenue of $442 million, up 26% year-over-year, and adjusted EBITDA of $89 million, with a 20% margin. Full year revenue was $1.635 billion, up 29%, and adjusted EBITDA reached $272 million, with a 17% margin. Net income for the year was $68 million, with $41 million in Q4 alone. Send volume for Q4 grew 35% to $21 billion, and send volume per active customer increased 13% year-over-year. Quarterly active customers rose 19% to nearly 9.3 million. Growth in high and very high amount senders was notable, with high amount sender volume up 14% and very high amount sender volume up 105% year-over-year.

  • U.S. revenue grew 28%, while Rest of World revenue increased 26%. Revenue from transactions outside India, the Philippines, and Mexico now comprises over half of the revenue mix.

  • New products contributed a little more than 1% to total revenue in 2025; this figure is expected to more than double in 2026.

  • Free cash flow for 2025 was $283 million, more than tripling from the prior year.

Q&A

  • Tien-Tsin Huang, JPMorgan: Asked Gunningham how his prior experience prepares him for Remitly. Gunningham replied, "Some of my prior experiences include leading product organizations and engineering organizations in some of the best companies in the world. Some of those were big, some of those were smaller. I've run large complex businesses in many continents. I've been a CEO a number of times."

  • Ramsey El-Assal, Cantor Fitzgerald: Questioned macro impacts like FX or immigration policy. Mehta responded, “Our customer is very resilient even in sort of geopolitical volatile background, which again strengthens our confidence with regards to the guidance.”

  • Aditya Buddhavarapu, BofA Securities: Inquired about revenue cadence and margin outlook for 2026. Mehta explained that Q1 benefits from the 1% remittance tax and that margin guidance for the full year is balanced between growth investments and ongoing efficiency.

  • William Nance, Goldman Sachs: Asked Gunningham about areas of focus as CEO. Gunningham emphasized product development velocity and addressing large markets.

  • Cristopher Kennedy, William Blair: Asked about AI opportunities. Gunningham replied, “There are a number of buckets of AI... the right AI adoption just gives us a lot of tailwinds into the next few years.”

  • Alexander Markgraff, KeyBanc: Asked about benefits from Flex and Remitly One. Mehta highlighted strong unit economics and higher spending among Flex users.

  • Darrin Peller, Wolfe Research: Asked about high amount senders. Mehta reported “north of 40%” growth for high amount senders and “north of 100%” growth for very high amount senders.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed appreciation for Oppenheimer’s leadership, welcomed Gunningham, and raised questions centered on growth drivers, macro impacts, and new products. The tone was slightly positive, with curiosity about sustainability and scalability.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in prepared remarks, using phrases like “I could not be more excited” and “We remain focused on long-term RLTE dollar growth.” In the Q&A, responses were measured, providing detail but deferring on specifics where appropriate, especially from Gunningham as new CEO.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more upbeat due to record results and a smooth leadership transition. Analysts were slightly more focused on execution risks and guidance details than before.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated to 29% for the full year from 25% in Q3. Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 17% for the year from 15% in Q3. Net income increased significantly, from $8.8 million in Q3 to $68 million for the year.

  • The strategic focus shifted to scaling new products and leveraging AI for operational efficiency. Guidance for 2026 revenue growth was set at 19%–20%, higher than the high-teens initial view last quarter. The leadership transition to a new CEO was the most significant management change this quarter.

  • Analysts’ focus shifted more toward the implications of new products and the impact of regulatory changes, whereas previous quarters were more concentrated on core business momentum and initial new product traction.

  • Management’s confidence was stronger, citing specific headwinds turning into tailwinds, such as the remittance tax and AI-driven efficiencies.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company acknowledged transaction loss rates may fluctuate quarter-to-quarter and highlighted ongoing macro and geopolitical uncertainties. Management plans to “manage costs thoughtfully” and invest in AI to mitigate operational risks. Analysts raised concerns about seasonality, the sustainability of high growth rates, and margin compression as new product investments ramp up.

Final Takeaway

Remitly concluded the call by reiterating its ambitions to transform cross-border financial services, supported by record revenue, expanding margins, and a robust product pipeline. With a strengthened leadership team, strategic investments in AI, and a clear path to scale new products, the company remains focused on accelerating growth, operational efficiency, and delivering on its vision to reach $3 billion in revenue and $600 million in adjusted EBITDA by 2028.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
